sections.paragraph:
ἐκβλαστάνω 1 aor. ἐξεβλάστησα (s. βλαστάνω; Hippocr., Alim. 6 ed. Litt. IX p. 100; Lucian, Ver. Hist. 2, 41) to spring up as a sprout, of seed, sprout up (so lit., Theophr., CP 1, 3, 5; 3, 23, 1; Num 17:20; Job 38:27; TestJos 19:6 [A]) Mk 4:5 v.l.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 300
